paper_title: The structure of binary matroids with no induced claw or Fano plane restriction
summary: The conjecture is verified for $s \in \{1,2,3\}$ with $k=1$ in the source paper (Corollary 5.2). Nelson and Nomoto (arXiv:2005.00089, SIAM JDM 2022) subsequently proved the case $s=4$, showing $I_4$-free and triangle-free binary matroids have critical number at most 2. The full conjecture for all $s \geq 5$ remains open.

since_posted.title: The structure of $I_4$-free and triangle-free binary matroids
since_posted.claim: Proves a decomposition theorem for $I_4$-free and triangle-free binary matroids, establishing that such matroids have critical number at most 2, thereby settling Conjecture 1.6 for the case $s=4$.

since_posted.title: The critical number of $I_{1,t}$-free triangle-free binary matroids
since_posted.claim: Proves that simple $I_{1,t}$-free and triangle-free binary matroids have bounded critical number for any $t \geq 1$, extending the bounded-critical-number programme to a broader family of excluded induced restrictions.

notes: Conjecture 1.6 is proved for s=1,2,3 (k=1) in the source paper and for s=4 (k=2) by Nelson–Nomoto 2020 (arXiv:2005.00089, SIAM JDM 2022). The companion paper arXiv:2011.06625 handles I_{1,t}-free variants, which may not directly address I_s for large s. The conjecture for s≥5 remains open.
